title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Share Certificates remains the largest funding component at 30.1% of total shares, totaling $254,828 thousand, providing a stable base for lending and liquidity needs while carrying fixed rates above regular share costs. Share Drafts accounts for $253,055 (29.9%), offering flexible, low-cost funding without maturity constraints or premium pricing structures. Regular Shares contribute $208,686 (24.7%) to the balance sheet as a core relationship-driven deposit line that typically carries lower rates than certificates but remains subject to member withdrawal preferences.

Share Drafts and Share Certificates both increased by 1.2% and 0.2% respectively versus the previous quarter, while Regular Shares grew at 3.2%. Money Market Shares rose marginally at 0.2%, whereas IRA/Keogh Accounts expanded slightly at 0.9%. All Other Shares climbed 6.9%, Nonmember Deposits remained flat at -0.0%, and no category contracted during the period.

The combined growth across Share Drafts, Share Certificates, Regular Shares, Money Market Shares, IRA/KEOGH ACCOUNTS, ALL OTHER SHARES indicates a broad-based expansion in funding sources without any single line dominating or retreating significantly from its prior position. This pattern suggests that Campus Credit Union maintained access to both low-cost flexible deposits and higher-yielding fixed-term instruments while avoiding overreliance on volatile categories.

Overall, the credit union’s deposit structure reflects balanced growth across multiple share types with no material shift toward either high-cost or inflexible funding sources. Management continues to focus on retaining core member relationships and managing the cost of the funding base.
